heading: Value Added Tax Act, 2025 (Act 1151) — section 3 rate and section 75 commencement slices

metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: Value Added Tax Act, 2025 (Act 1151) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07 (88-page consolidated Act; scanned print, OCR at 300 dpi). This entry promotes two slices: section 3 (print page 6 — "the rate of the Tax is fifteen per cent and is calculated on the value of the taxable supply of the goods or services; or import") and section 75 (print page 71 — "This Act shall come into force on the 1st day of January, 2026"). The full zero-rating/exemption schedules remain in the print for later slices; the Act replaces the Value Added Tax Act, 2013 (Act 870) chain from 2026.
metadata.title: Value Added Tax Act, 2025 (Act 1151) — section 3 rate and section 75 commencement slices

citation_label: Ghana Education Trust Fund (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1152) — section 3A levy substitution
heading: Ghana Education Trust Fund (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1152) — section 3A levy substitution

metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: Ghana Education Trust Fund (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1152) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07. Substitutes section 3A of the Ghana Education Trust Fund Act, 2000 (Act 581): the Ghana Education Trust Fund Levy at a rate of two and a half per cent, paid at the time the goods or services are supplied. The window captures the section 3A substitution only.
metadata.title: Ghana Education Trust Fund (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1152) — section 3A levy substitution

body: 2. The principal enactment is amended by the substitution for section 3A, of “Imposition of Ghana Education Trust Fund Levy 3A. (1) There is imposed by this Act, a Ghana Education Trust Fund Levy on the (a) supply of goods or services made in the country other than exempt goods or services; and (b) import of goods or services other than exempt imports. (2) The rate of the Levy is two and a half per cent. (3) The Levy shall be paid at the time the goods or services are supplied or imported. (4) Except as otherwise provided in this Act, the Levy is charged on the supply of goods or services where the supply is (a) a taxable supply; and (b) made by a taxable person in the course of the taxable activity of that person.”.
citation_label: Ghana Education Trust Fund (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1152) — section 3A levy substitution 1
heading: section-3a-getfund-levy-substituted

citation_label: National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1156) — section 47 levy substitution
heading: National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1156) — section 47 levy substitution

metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1156) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07. Substitutes section 47 of the National Health Insurance Act, 2012 (Act 852): the National Health Insurance Levy at a rate of two and a half per cent, with twenty per cent of the revenue allocated to the Ghana Medical Trust Fund. The window captures the section 47 substitution only; sections 48-51 (input-deduction, zero-rating, reliefs, collection) remain in the print for later slices.
metadata.title: National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1156) — section 47 levy substitution

body: 1. The National Health Insurance Act, 2012 (Act 852), referred to in this Act as the “principal enactment” is amended by the substitution for section 47, of “Imposition of National Health Insurance Levy 47. (1) There is imposed by this Act, a National Health Insurance Levy on the (a) supply of goods or services made in the country, other than exempt goods or services; and (b) import of goods or services, other than exempt imports. 2 National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 Act 1156 (2) The rate of the Levy is two and a half per cent. (3) The Levy shall be paid at the time the goods or services are supplied or imported. (4) Except as otherwise provided in this Act, die Levy is charged on the supply of goods or services where the supply is (a) a taxable supply; and (b) made by a taxable person in the course of the taxable activity of that person. (5) Parliament shall allocate twenty per cent of the revenue generated from the National Health Insurance Levy to the Ghana Medical Trust Fund.”.
citation_label: National Health Insurance (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1156) — section 47 levy substitution 1
heading: section-47-nhil-substituted
